findings.evidence: Lines 88-94 define the elimination; grep of the file shows no lemma whose conclusion is `IsPositiveDefiniteKernel K` taking quadratic-form/Hermitian data as hypotheses — only the def (l.58), the rank-one constructor (l.64), and closure lemmas (add/mul/smul/comp).
findings.fix: Add the introduction lemma (ideally as an `iff` to complete the characterization): from conjugate symmetry of K together with `∀ n v x, 0 ≤ ∑ i, ∑ j, conj (x i) * x j * K (v i) (v j)`, conclude `IsPositiveDefiniteKernel K`. This lets the F(a+b⋆) construction and other consumers build PD kernels without unfolding Matrix.PosSemidef.
findings.issue: sum_conj_mul_mul_nonneg gives the quadratic-form elimination (PD ⇒ ∑ conj xᵢ xⱼ K ≥ 0) but there is no introduction counterpart. The only ways to prove IsPositiveDefiniteKernel are the raw Fin n PosSemidef definition or the rank-one constructor plus closures; the stated next target K(a,b)=F(a+b⋆) is not such a mixture, so a consumer must drop to Matrix.PosSemidef and re-derive Hermitian + form nonneg by hand.

summary: Surface is minimal and bodies are hidden, and elimination/closure coverage is good, but the characteristic API is incomplete: there is a quadratic-form elimination lemma with no matching introduction, forcing the named K(a,b)=F(a+b⋆) target to unfold to Matrix.PosSemidef.
